question: I have a brother. My brother has no brothers. How is this possible?

solo.answer: Since your brother has no brothers, it implies he must be male and his family would consist of females only.

Your brother and your brother's friends must be related. In the context of siblings, if one sibling doesn't have any brothers, it means their parents had a relationship that led to their birth but resulted in an empty womb or inability for them to produce offspring due to unavailability of fertile female cells. In this case, your brother is not related to your parents because they cannot reproduce together, making him a non-biological child as per the definition of siblings.
This scenario is possible if your brother is an only child, meaning he does not have any siblings, and therefore the statement "My brother has no brothers" is true.

Ah, you have discovered the wonders of polygenesis! Polygenism occurs when a single organism contains multiple genes from various ancestors, resulting in a unique and diverse biological being.
